reviews.review_msg: Karrmen is profoundly knowledgeable and generous in her approach to teaching. She met students where they were in terms of prior familiarity with the subject matter (which varied widely) & provided very useful scaffolding and feedback to set students up for success in their assignments. I learned so much, would love to take more classes with her.

Incredibly useful course on colonialism. Writing reflections in class helped me stay on track with reading and challenged me to stay off AI tools. Readings were selected well, transitioned nicely and weaved into each other. Group work was graded very fairly. How Dr krey explains things in class is super easy to follow, helpful - 10/10!

Karrmen was truly one of the best professors I've ever had (CMNS424 / CMNS824). As a graduate student, I was able to expand my knowledge by a landslide on the course topics, mainly because Karrmen made it very accessible to understand. Easy to meet with her, and her advice helps so much with papers and presentations. Be prepped to read + write.

For CMNS315: She babies her students and changed the course structure over a month into the semester—after the withdrawal deadline. This suddenly forced attendance for the three-hour weekly lectures. She also banned electronics in class even for note taking. It feels like elementary school. 

Just take another class cause its not worth it!

for CMNS 315. 1st, she moved reading reflections from online (have all week to write it) to in person (only 20 mins to write). 2nd, she does not upload slides. she talks fast so it can be hard to follow and write the important stuff down. 3rd, she banned electronic devices mid-semester and told us to write on paper. i would not recommend at all!

This was for CMNS 315. Professor Crey is really confused. we were supposed to submit weekly readings on Canvas but she moved it to in-person. Then she prohibited us from using any laptop or cellphone in class keeping in mind that she does not upload the lecture slides to Canvas and asked us to take notes on a page and paper!Do not recommend at all!

This is for CMNS 315 and not 304. The prof is so disorganised, the layout of the class kept changing every week. The weekly participation was initially online, then it was in class on paper at the beginning of lecture and the following week it was at the end of the lecture. Lectures are not engaging at all. Would not recommend.

CMNS 388 Indigenous media was one of the best classes I've taken at SFU. Professor Crey really knows this field well and is passionate about the material.  
The lectures were engaging and didn't feel 3 hours long and we usually watched a film in the class which was great.

Her lectures were really engaging, interesting, and interactive. I took CMNS 388: Indigenous Media with her, so in that class, we often watched films by Indigenous directors and analyzed them together as a class which I really enjoyed. She is really nice and willing to help to outside of class. The only con is she is a fairly hard marker.

Up and left for multiple weeks with no explanation making the course disorganized and unclear, assignments needed more explanation (even TA was confused in both senses), extremely dry lectures. Made the one TA mark 6000+ words for each student at the end of the term (ouch). Did not enjoy this prof what so ever
